Neither is this prospect.

## Call Script

[OPENER — 1-2 lines]
Hey, is this JJ, or someone with JJ's Painting Company over on Brown Road in Stockbridge?
This is Padre with Executive Leverage (R&D) — I work with local trades, and I noticed you've built a strong reputation painting homes around Henry County but don't have a real website pulling that together anywhere.

[VALUE — 2 sentences]
So I went ahead and built you a sample landing page — your interior and exterior work, your reviews, and a tap-to-call button — already live at https://hitthe.link/padre/jjs-painting/.
I just took the public info about the company and turned it into something a homeowner can actually find on their phone and act on.

[ASK]
Could I borrow 5 minutes to walk you through what I put together? I can text you the link right now and you'll see it on your phone in a second.

[SOFT CLOSE — price anchor + deposit]
The full site — yours to own outright, hosted, with your logo and any tweaks you want — runs $1,500. We do half ($750) as a deposit to get started, the rest when we go live on your own domain. Most trades are live within 48 hours.
[Soft close question]
Does it make sense to get you set up this week or next?

## Value bullets (for any talking-point moment)

- You've earned a strong word-of-mouth reputation painting homes in Stockbridge, but right now it lives on scattered directory pages — Yelp, Nextdoor. A single clean site puts it all front and center where a homeowner lands first.
- One tap → the homeowner calls you directly from any phone. Someone searching "house painter near me" in Henry County won't bounce to a competitor while hunting for your number.
- The contact form captures a quote request while you're up on a ladder. Every missed call right now is a repaint a competing crew is booking instead of you.
- The site listed for you online ("jjpaintpro.club") is dead — it refuses to load. A homeowner who clicks it sees nothing and moves on. This replaces it with a page that actually works.
- Interior, exterior, pressure washing, deck and fence work — the site spells all of it out up front, so the people who call already know you do the job they need.

---

## Objection handling

**I already stay busy on word-of-mouth.**
→ Totally — that reputation is exactly the asset here. The site catches the homeowners who heard your name but Googled before calling, and the ones comparing two painters on their phone. That's the slice you never see book.

**I don't have time to deal with a website.**
→ That's the whole point — it's already built. You don't manage it, you don't update it. Maybe a half hour of your time total: glance at the copy, swap a photo if you want, then we publish.

**I think I already have a website.**
→ I looked — the one listed for you, jjpaintpro.club, won't load at all right now; it just refuses to connect. So to a homeowner clicking it, you effectively have no site. This gives you one that actually opens, on your own domain.

**$1,500 is too much.**
→ Fair question. What's an average repaint job worth to you? One job you'd have otherwise missed covers the whole site — and you own it outright, no monthly fee. After that it just works for you.

**I'm not sure that's even my right info.**
→ Good instinct — and I'd rather get it exact than guess. Whatever your true phone, service area, and reviews are, we'll confirm them with you before anything goes live. Nothing publishes that you haven't signed off on.
